page: Microsoft Azure E128-64s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv6 server family. It features an Intel processor with 64 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 1024.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-CPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, positioning the instance within the memory-optimized category. The virtualization layer is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server. Additionally, the instance features no graphics processing units (GPUs), which limits its utility for hardware-accelerated rendering or deep learning workloads.

The qualitative cost profile of the E128-64s_v6 reflects its high memory density relative to the allocated compute cores. This resource balance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require a proportional scaling of CPU cores. Appropriate workloads for this configuration include large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise resource planning (ERP) applications, and memory-bound data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure E128-64s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Esv6 family, powered by an Intel processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage, requiring external Premium Storage options, and contains no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without a corresponding increase in compute cores. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is centered on its high memory density, making it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and data analytics workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and one terabyte of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
1024.0 GB of system memory (16.0 GB per core)
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Designed for in-memory databases and caching layers
